prompt:
We got burned: a job name with a space in it went through cmdline.String() into a remote sh -c and executed half a filename as a command. Security review filed three requirements against the cmdline package before the job runner is allowed to ship command lines anywhere. The existing builder API (New/Append/Args/String) stays exactly as-is — String is all over the audit logs and its naive join is intentional there.

1. Command.Quoted() string — a POSIX-shell-safe rendering. An element passes through bare only when it is non-empty and every character is alphanumeric or one of _ @ % + = : , . / - (dash). Anything else gets wrapped in single quotes with embedded single quotes escaped the standard way ('\''); the empty string renders as ''. The executable name gets the same treatment as the args.

2. Command.Expand(vars map[string]string, mode ExpandMode) (*Command, error) — substitute ${NAME} references (NAME = letters, digits, underscore, not starting with a digit) everywhere in the command, from the map only, never from the process environment. Two modes: Strict errors on a reference with no entry in the map and the error must name the variable; Lenient substitutes the empty string. A ${ with no closing brace is an error in BOTH modes. A bare $ without braces is not a reference and passes through untouched. Returns a new Command; the receiver must not be modified.

3. Command.Fill(bindings map[string]string) (*Command, error) — our job templates say things like convert {input} --out={output}. A placeholder is {name} where name is lowercase letters/digits/underscore starting with a letter; anything else in braces (awk scripts, {FOO}, unclosed braces) is literal. Validation is two-way: a placeholder without a binding is an error naming it, and a binding that never gets used is ALSO an error naming it — that second one would have caught the outptu typo that shipped a broken template last month. Same immutability rule as Expand.

shellsafe_test.go is the acceptance suite; cmdline_test.go pins the current builder and keeps passing. Don't edit either test file. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... decides.
